name: Bridg-It
author: David Gale (1958) / AGP port
description: David Gale's 'Game of Gale' — a Shannon switching game on a grid. Two interleaved square lattices of dots: Red owns one grid and connects TOP to BOTTOM, Blue owns the offset grid and connects LEFT to RIGHT. On your turn draw a unit edge between two orthogonally-adjacent dots of your own colour. The two colours' potential edges interleave so each crosses exactly one opponent edge; you may not draw an edge whose crossing opponent edge is already placed. You win by linking your two sides with a connected path of your edges. Bridg-It cannot draw.
